title: saxifraga granulata subsp. corsica
intro: Saxifraga granulata subsp. corsica, also known as the Corsican saxifrage, is a subspecies of the Saxifraga granulata plant, a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Saxifragaceae family. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties. The Corsican saxifrage has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as wounds, skin irritations, and digestive problems, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. It can be prepared as an herbal tea or tincture, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
